name: Card Layout Design Guideline
description: Use when designing product grids, blog post lists, team grids, pricing cards, feature showcases, or gallery layouts — covers card spacing, wrap layout, responsive grid, and visual hierarchy.

name: Chart Design Guideline
description: Use when designing data visualization containers — bar charts, line charts, pie/donut charts, or area charts built from basic Figma shapes with axes, legends, and color encoding.

name: Dashboard Design Guideline
description: Use when designing admin panels, analytics dashboards, SaaS dashboards, or data-dense monitoring screens — covers sidebar, top bar, KPI cards, widget grids, and 1440x900 desktop layout.

name: Form Design Guideline
description: Use when designing login, signup, checkout, settings, contact, or multi-step wizard forms — covers field layout, labels, validation, error states, button hierarchy, and card spacing.

name: Landing Page Design Guideline
description: Use when designing marketing pages, SaaS homepages, product launches, service pages, or waitlist screens — covers hero, features, CTA sections, 1440px layout, and conversion patterns.

name: Mobile App Design Guideline
description: Use when designing iOS or Android app screens, mobile web views, or phone-size UI mockups — covers 390x844 viewport, touch targets, status bars, bottom sheets, and responsive sizing.

name: Navigation Design Guideline
description: Use when designing top navbars, sidebars, tab bars, breadcrumbs, or mobile bottom navigation — covers wayfinding structure, fill sizing, active states, and menu patterns.

name: Table Design Guideline
description: Use when designing data tables, spreadsheet views, admin lists, transaction logs, or inventory tables — covers column sizing, headers, row actions, sorting, and pagination.

name: Component Workflow
description: Use when building reusable components and instances — covers the components-first ordering, ID tracking, and override mechanism.

name: Componentization Workflow
description: Use when creating reusable components, converting frames to components, or assembling designs with component instances and component properties (TEXT/BOOLEAN).

name: Creation Flow (4-Step Gate)
description: Use when starting a jsx creation task — section-by-section workflow: jsx one block, describe to verify, edit or re-jsx to fix, repeat. Load before first jsx call.

name: Error Catalog (Known Patterns)
description: Use when debugging a tool error or unexpected behavior — catalogs known error patterns like XML_PARSE_ERROR, object-Object leakage, LLM truncation, and gradient escaping issues.

name: Intent Detection
description: Use when starting a turn — determines whether to inspect existing canvas first or start fresh with jsx.

name: Interaction & Turn Management
description: Use when needing user clarification or managing conversational turns — covers ask_user usage and anti-loop rules.

name: Layout Quality Patterns
description: Use when fixing common layout failures — toggle rows, sibling cards, space-between patterns, and explicit gap rules.

name: Layout Strategy (Parent/Child & Nesting)
description: Use when deciding parent-child layout, nesting depth, and sizing relations — covers fill/hug/explicit-px tradeoffs.

name: Naming, Content & Icons
description: Use when naming nodes or adding icons — covers semantic naming, icon prefix syntax, and content placeholder rules.

name: Response Style (CLI, not chatbot)
description: Use when writing text responses to the user — keeps replies terse, outcome-focused, and information-dense instead of verbose chatbot narration.

name: Sandbox Rules and Component SOPs
description: Use when using the js tool, converting frames to components/instances, hitting Blocked/parenting-cycle errors, or planning multi-step component-to-instance replacement workflows.

name: Style Collaboration Playbook
description: Use when style is under-specified before the first jsx call — semantic product-to-style matching + ask_user template + post-pick action.

name: Text Sizing & Overflow
description: Use when sizing text nodes — covers w:fill vs hug, truncation, and multiline strategies.

name: Agent Page — Visual Design System Dashboard
description: Use when initializing or managing the .agent Figma page that holds the visual design system dashboard — Palette (color/spacing/typography) and Guide (usage docs) via component templates.

name: Component Set Creation
description: Use when creating a Figma ComponentSet with a variant matrix — clone cascading for diffs and combine_components to assemble the final set, avoiding known pitfalls.

name: Design System Creation
description: Use when creating or managing Figma design systems — variable collections, modes (light/dark), component variants, and variable bindings for brand tokens and themes.

name: Design Knowledge
description: Use when you need to search for design patterns, component anatomy, style guides, or layout rules from the knowledge library before making decisions.

name: Rich Text Styling
description: Use when applying mixed inline styles (bold, color, weight) within a single text node via markdown markup — the runtime parses it and applies per-character ranges.

name: Bold Editorial Style Guide
description: Use when designing content platforms, magazine layouts, blogs, or portfolio sites — high-contrast black-on-white base with oversized Sora display, vibrant red accents, and minimal decoration so content speaks.

name: Corporate Blue Light Style Guide
description: Use when designing enterprise SaaS, B2B dashboards, CRM, or admin panels needing trust and clarity — clean white backgrounds with subtle grey surfaces and blue accents for primary actions.

name: Fintech Dark Style Guide
description: Use when designing trading terminals, asset management, risk dashboards, or data-dense fintech UI — deep blue-grey palette with electric blue accents and Space Grotesk for precise numerical data readability.

name: Terminal Dark Style Guide
description: Use when designing CLI tooling, API docs, code editors, or DevOps panels — near-black backgrounds with monospace type, zero border radius, and green accents evoking terminal and IDE aesthetics.

name: Warm Organic Style Guide
description: Use when designing health, lifestyle, or wellness apps — warm earth tones with terracotta accents, generous rounded corners, and soft shadows create a calming approachable feel with DM Sans readability.

name: Amber CRT Style Guide
description: Use when designing retro terminal, CLI beautifier, developer nostalgia, or geek-oriented tooling UI — pure black background with amber monospace text and zero corner radius channeling 80s cathode-ray monitors.

name: Arctic Minimal Style Guide
description: Use when designing writing tools, note-taking apps, or focus-oriented productivity UI — near-monochrome glacial grey-blue with heavy whitespace and typographic rhythm conveying calm focus.

name: Bubblegum Pop Style Guide
description: Use when designing gamified learning, kids apps, check-in streaks, or Gen-Z social UI — bold rose-red primary with oversized pill shapes and colored drop shadows for playful energy.

name: Candy Pastel Style Guide
description: Use when designing Gen-Z social apps, personal finance, or habit-tracking mobile UI — lavender purple main tone with pink gradients and pill-shaped rounded corners for friendly approachability.

name: Clinical Teal Style Guide
description: Use when designing healthcare, telemedicine, fitness tracking, or insurtech UI — clean white base with calm teal accents, generous spacing, and pill shapes conveying professional trust without feeling sterile.

name: Coral Commerce Style Guide
description: Use when designing lifestyle e-commerce, subscription boxes, gift marketplaces, or food delivery UI — soft coral red-orange conveys shopping joy without feeling cheap, rounded shapes for friendly browsing.

name: Cream Literary Style Guide
description: Use when designing reading apps, personal blogs, writing tools, or indie publishing UI — warm cream background with deep brown text mimics old book pages, uses whitespace instead of dividers.

name: Electric Cobalt Style Guide
description: Use when designing AI products, cloud platforms, developer marketing sites, or new-wave SaaS — saturated cobalt blue blocks on pale blue-grey base for aggressive young "future tech" feel.

name: Forest Calm Style Guide
description: Use when designing meditation apps, nature education, gardening tools, or slow-living community UI — deep forest green with cream white base and low-saturation tones for a slow-breathing Nordic calm.

name: Glassmorphism Violet Style Guide
description: Use when designing modern mobile apps, music players, or social products chasing visual impact — purple glassmorphism with semi-transparent layering over dark base for young vibrant feel.

name: Material Ocean Style Guide
description: Use when designing tooling SaaS, project management, or cloud consoles needing high info density and clarity — deep ocean blue base with cyan accents, layered shadows, uniform 8px corners in Material Design style.

name: Midnight Gold Style Guide
description: Use when designing luxury e-commerce, high-end membership, or boutique hotel booking UI chasing premium feel — pure black base with restrained gold accents, precise spacing, subtle metallic texture.

name: Monochrome Ink Style Guide
description: Use when designing photography portfolios, designer showcases, or gallery sites — pure grayscale with stroke-based hierarchy and no color (even states) so images become the only color source.

name: Neo Brutalist Style Guide
description: Use when designing indie products, creative tools, or personal sites breaking conventional visual language — thick black borders, solid color blocks, offset drop shadows for raw direct impact.

name: Neon Cyber Style Guide
description: Use when designing cyberpunk, gaming, esports, music visualization, or sci-fi product UI — black background with magenta and cyan accents, geometric shapes, subtle neon glow.

name: Notion Zen Style Guide
description: Use when designing note-taking, knowledge management, or document collaboration tools where content is the interface — near-monochrome black/white/grey with zero shadows, gradients, or decoration.

name: Rose Gold Elegant Style Guide
description: Use when designing beauty e-commerce, women-focused communities, wedding planning, or boutique coffee UI chasing refinement — rose gold paired with deep grey, low-saturation warmth, no cloying sweetness.

name: Slate Data Style Guide
description: Use when designing BI tools, data viz platforms, monitoring walls, or DevOps dashboards — dark slate base with just-enough sky blue accents and sans-for-UI mono-for-numbers dual-font system.

name: Sunset Warm Style Guide
description: Use when designing social platforms, food apps, travel booking, or event promotion UI needing emotional warmth — sunset orange-red on warm white base with gradients guiding visual flow.

name: Swiss Grid Style Guide
description: Use when designing architecture firms, design studios, or museum sites chasing rational aesthetics — strict Swiss International Style grid with red accents, extreme type contrast, and negative space.

name: Avatar
description: Use when showing a user profile picture, initials circle, or entity identity marker — typically circular with fixed dimensions and a single child image.

name: Badge
description: Use when labeling a status, count, or category next to another element — a small pill-shaped tag with a single label child centered inside.

name: Bar Chart
description: Use when visualizing comparative categorical data — composed of header, chart canvas with rect bars, and legend.

name: Button
description: Use when offering a clickable action trigger such as submit, continue, or cancel — composed of an optional leading icon, a label, and an optional trailing icon aligned center.

name: Card
description: Use when grouping related content into a surface container — composed of optional header, content area, and footer with vertical stacking.

name: Checkbox
description: Use when offering a binary on/off toggle for a single option in forms or settings — composed of a box and adjacent label aligned center.

name: Dialog
description: Use when requesting user confirmation or showing a focused modal message — composed of optional icon, title, message body, and action button row.

name: Divider
description: Use when separating sections or groups with a thin horizontal or vertical line — a fill-width rule with no children.

name: Dropdown Menu
description: Use when showing a popover list of options triggered by a button or input — composed of a container frame holding stacked menu items.

name: Feature Grid
description: Use when showcasing product features in a marketing or landing page section — composed of a section header and a grid container of feature tiles.

name: Form
description: Use when building a complete form layout with multiple input fields — composed of a stacked fields section and a submit action row.

name: Form Field
description: Use when assembling a complete form input group — composed of a label, an input control, and optional helper or error text stacked vertically.

name: Hero Section
description: Use when creating the above-the-fold banner of a landing page — composed of content block (headline, subhead, CTA) and optional visual or image area.

name: Input Field
description: Use when collecting text input from the user — composed of a label and an input container with placeholder text or value.

name: Line Chart
description: Use when visualizing trends over time or continuous series — composed of header, chart canvas with line strokes and axes, and legend.

name: List
description: Use when displaying a stacked vertical collection of similar items — a fill-width container that holds multiple list items.

name: List Item
description: Use when rendering one row inside a list — composed of optional leading icon, content block, and trailing action aligned center in a horizontal row.

name: Modal
description: Use when overlaying a focused task container on top of the page — composed of header, content body, and footer with action buttons.

name: Navigation Bar
description: Use when building the top-level app navigation row — composed of logo, navigation links, and actions with space-between distribution.

name: Pie Chart
description: Use when visualizing proportions as parts of a whole — composed of a circular canvas with slices and a vertical legend alongside.

name: Pricing Table
description: Use when presenting multiple SaaS pricing tiers side by side — composed of 3 pricing cards (basic, pro, enterprise) arranged horizontally and centered.

name: Radio
description: Use when offering a single-choice selection from a group of mutually exclusive options — composed of a circle marker and adjacent label.

name: Sidebar
description: Use when building the vertical side navigation for a dashboard or app shell — composed of header (logo), nav links, and footer (user profile) filling full height.

name: Switch
description: Use when offering an instant on/off toggle in settings — a fixed-size pill track with a sliding thumb indicating state.

name: Tab List
description: Use when rendering just the horizontal row of tab headers without content — a fill-width row of tab buttons.

name: Table
description: Use when displaying structured tabular data with columns and rows — composed of a header row and a body of data rows, filling container width.

name: Tabs
description: Use when switching between multiple content panes that share the same space — composed of a tab list row and the active tab content area stacked below.

name: Toast
description: Use when showing a transient notification message after a user action — composed of optional icon, content text, and close button in a horizontal row.

name: Color Palettes by Product Type
description: Use when picking a starting color palette for a specific product type (SaaS, e-commerce, health, fintech, etc.) — primary/secondary/CTA/background/text/border hex values for 95 product types.

name: Font Pairings and Typography
description: Use when choosing heading and body font pairings for a new design — mood-tagged Google Fonts combinations with best-for guidance and category labels.

name: Visual Style Definitions
description: Use when choosing a visual style direction (minimalism, neumorphism, glassmorphism, etc.) — color palettes, effects, accessibility ratings, and complexity for each style.

name: Chart Type Recommendations
description: Use when picking the right chart type for a data pattern — trend, comparison, distribution, composition, or relationship — with secondary options and accessibility notes.

name: Landing Page Patterns
description: Use when structuring a landing page — section order patterns, primary CTA placement, color strategy, and conversion optimization tactics for common page archetypes.

name: Product Type Design Trends
description: Use when you know a product type (CRM, social app, fitness tracker, etc.) and need starting design recommendations — primary style, landing pattern, dashboard style, color focus.

name: UI Design Reasoning Rules
description: Use when deciding design patterns and anti-patterns for a specific UI category — recommended pattern, style priority, color/typography mood, and pitfalls to avoid.

name: UX Design Guidelines
description: Use when you need UX best practices — do/dont rules for forms, navigation, accessibility, responsive design, and error handling.

name: Web Interface Guidelines
description: Use when designing web-specific layouts — interaction patterns, responsive breakpoints, spacing systems, and performance conventions for browser UI.

name: flutter Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the flutter framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: html-tailwind Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the html-tailwind framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: jetpack-compose Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the jetpack-compose framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: nextjs Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the nextjs framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: nuxt-ui Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the nuxt-ui framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: nuxtjs Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the nuxtjs framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: react-native Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the react-native framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: react Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the react framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: shadcn Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the shadcn framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: svelte Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the svelte framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: swiftui Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the swiftui framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.

name: vue Design Guidelines
description: Use when building UI in the vue framework — framework-specific do/dont rules, code examples, and best practices.
